reasoning: The Lean signature faithfully captures Rudin's exercise. Both spaces are typed as `MetricSpace`, matching "metric space X" and "metric space Y". The hypotheses `Continuous f`, `Continuous g` correspond to "continuous mappings". `Dense s` in Mathlib unfolds to `∀ x, x ∈ closure s`, which is exactly "P is a dense subset of X". The hypothesis `∀ x ∈ s, f x = g x` matches "g(p) = f(p) for all p ∈ P" (equality is symmetric). The conclusion `f = g` is, by function extensionality, equivalent to "g(p) = f(p) for all p ∈ X". No extra hypotheses (such as nonemptiness or completeness) have been added, and none have been dropped.
